security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.
An attacker can craft a valid vote message without valid authorization tokens

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be altered without proper authorization.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering requires access to the voting interface and some understanding of the contract.

assumptions: The governance contract does not have sufficient input validation to prevent tampering.
The actor can modify the vote message before submission.
The system lacks mechanisms to detect or prevent vote tampering

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust input validation and integrity checks on the governance contract.

description: An attacker may alter the vote message to change the outcome of the governance proposal.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes may not be reliably attributed to users.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Lack of proper logging and auditing mechanisms for votes make it difficult to trace the origin of actions.

assumptions: The governance contract does not provide sufficient logging and auditing mechanisms to track votes.
There is no mechanism to securely associate the vote with the user’s identity.
Vote data is not cryptographically signed or otherwise tamper-evident

mitigation.strategy: Implement detailed logging and auditing for all governance actions, including user IDs and timestamps. Integrate cryptographic signing or other methods for tamper-evident data.

description: An attacker can cast a vote and deny their participation.
